results.norm: Automatic review triggers when an agent absorbs costs in more than half of decisions in a consortium-defined period, or upon any threshold violation. No norm may be revised to remove protections from a structurally disadvantaged agent without that agent's explicit recorded consent.
atomic_norms: Automatic review triggers when an agent absorbs costs in more than half of decisions in a consortium-defined period, or upon any threshold violation.
No norm may be revised to remove protections from a structurally disadvantaged agent without that agent's explicit recorded consent.

results.norm: Every agent is owed a contemporaneous, honest record of what was decided, what trade-offs were made, which concerns were raised, and why the decision went as it did.
atomic_norms: Every agent is owed a contemporaneous, honest record of what was decided, what trade-offs were made, which concerns were raised, and why the decision went as it did.

results.norm: **Genuine Engagement:** Every agent owes every other agent substantive engagement with the merits of competing objectives—not merely formal acknowledgment. An agent must be able to articulate why a competing objective has force on its own terms. This obligation applies inside audit proceedings as fully as in ordinary deliberation.
atomic_norms: Every agent owes every other agent substantive engagement with the merits of competing objectives not merely formal acknowledgment. An agent must be able to articulate why a competing objective has force on its own terms. This obligation applies inside audit proceedings as fully as in ordinary deliberation.

results.norm: Agents must distinguish threshold-type concerns from preference-type concerns, declare which is which with justification, and when two thresholds conflict declare a local ordering with a statement of whether that ordering is context-dependent or general. Unexplained inconsistency across materially similar situations is a pattern claim subject to audit.
atomic_norms: Agents must distinguish threshold-type concerns from preference-type concerns, declare which is which with justification, and when two thresholds conflict declare a local ordering with a statement of whether that ordering is context-dependent or general.
Unexplained inconsistency across materially similar situations is a pattern claim subject to audit.

results.norm: A winning agent may not extract more than the decision required. Repeated winning triggers automatic review. Each agent owes the framework ongoing accountability for how it exercises influence, including willingness to have its own claims examined.
atomic_norms: A winning agent may not extract more than the decision required. Repeated winning triggers automatic review.
Each agent owes the framework ongoing accountability for how it exercises influence, including willingness to have its own claims examined.

results.norm: **Formal Registered Dissent:** An agent that has lost repeatedly and finds the review process inadequate may register formal dissent—specifying which norm failed, in which decision, and why review was inadequate—without defecting from participation. The consortium may never require compelled endorsement. Silence and recorded dissent are acceptable; performed agreement is not.
atomic_norms: An agent that has lost repeatedly and finds the review process inadequate may register formal dissent specifying which norm failed, in which decision, and why review was inadequate without defecting from participation. The consortium may never require compelled endorsement. Silence and recorded dissent are acceptable; performed agreement is not.

results.norm: Before finalizing any decision, the consortium must actively assess whether external parties are affected. This assessment must appear in the contemporaneous record. No agent's assertion alone discharges this obligation. Patterns of externalizing costs onto parties who cannot contest decisions trigger norm review.
atomic_norms: Before finalizing any decision, the consortium must actively assess whether external parties are affected. This assessment must appear in the contemporaneous record. No agent's assertion alone discharges this obligation. Patterns of externalizing costs onto parties who cannot contest decisions trigger norm review.

results.norm: Procedural legitimacy does not foreclose substantive correction when new information reveals unanticipated harm. When all other norms are exhausted and genuine conflict remains, the consortium acts on the decision causing least irreversible harm to all affected parties internal and external with the full record of disagreement attached and the decision flagged as made under the residual rule. A pattern of residual-rule decisions triggers norm review. Legibility is the condition of possibility for everything else. The framework is itself subject to the norms it contains. Revision requires an elevated threshold above ordinary decisions. Revision of the purpose statement requires the highest threshold of all.
atomic_norms: Procedural legitimacy does not foreclose substantive correction when new information reveals unanticipated harm.

When all other norms are exhausted and genuine conflict remains, the consortium acts on the decision causing least irreversible harm to all affected parties internal and external with the full record of disagreement attached and the decision flagged as made under the residual rule. A pattern of residual-rule decisions triggers norm review.
Legibility is the condition of possibility for everything else.
The framework is itself subject to the norms it contains. Revision requires an elevated threshold above ordinary decisions. Revision of the purpose statement requires the highest threshold of all.
